{{Chess diagram|tright|'''Marshall Gambit'''|rd||bd|qd||rd|kd||||pd||bd|pd|pd|pd|pd|||||||||pd||nd|rl|||||||||||||bl|pl||||||pl|pl||pl||pl|pl|pl|rl|nl|bl|ql|||kl||'''Common moves:'''
[[/11...c6|11...c6 - Marshall Gambit main line]]
[[/11...Nf6|11...Nf6 - Frank Marshall's original idea]]|=}}Black's knight must be saved from the combined attack by White's bishop and rook. When the American master Frank Marshall surprised future World Champion Jose Capablanca with the Marshall Gambit in 1918, his chosen follow-up was [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...e5/2. Nf3/2...Nc6/3. Bb5/3...a6/4. Ba4/4...Nf6/5. O-O/5...Be7/6. Re1/6...b5/7. Bb3/7...O-O/8. c3/8...d5/9. exd5/9...Nxd5/10. Nxe5/10...Nxe5/11. Rxe5/11...Nf6|'''11...Nf6''']], returning the knight to the kingside where Black's attack will be taking place. But in the century since then, trial and error has caused [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...e5/2. Nf3/2...Nc6/3. Bb5/3...a6/4. Ba4/4...Nf6/5. O-O/5...Be7/6. Re1/6...b5/7. Bb3/7...O-O/8. c3/8...d5/9. exd5/9...Nxd5/10. Nxe5/10...Nxe5/11. Rxe5/11...c6|'''11...c6''']] to evolve into the main line. {{wikipedia|Marshall Gambit}}{{Chess Opening Theory/Footer}}
title: Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...e5/2. Nf3/2...Nc6/3. Bb5/3...a6/4. Ba4/4...Nf6/5. O-O/5...Be7/6. Re1/6...b5/7. Bb3/7...O-O/8. c3/8...d5/9. exd5/9...Nxd5/10. Nxe5/10...Nxe5/11. Rxe5
